description: This figure presents the deformation-induced martensitic transformation kinetics in a low-density medium manganese steel (Fe-7.95Mn-0.25C-4.94Al wt.%) during tensile deformation along two perpendicular directions. The plot shows martensite fraction (vol.%) as a function of true strain (0–0.5), comparing experimental data from synchrotron high-energy X-ray diffraction (HEXRD) with predictions from the Olson-Cohen (OC) model. Three curves are displayed: Exp.RD (red circles) for the rolling direction, Exp.TD (blue circles) for the transverse direction, and Model (dashed black lines) representing fitted curves. The RD sample exhibits significantly faster transformation kinetics, reaching ~25% martensite at 0.5 strain compared to ~20% in the TD sample. The OC model (with parameters n=2 and initial austenite fraction of 46.5 vol.%) accurately captures both datasets by incorporating a strain partitioning factor of 1.15 (ε_RD = 1.15 ε_TD), demonstrating that loading direction-dependent strain distribution governs the anisotropic transformation behavior.
